McCulloch-Pitts Neuron (Detailed Hinglish Notes)

1. Introduction

  • Ab hum biological neuron se computational neuron model ki  taraf move kar rahe hain.

  • Sabse pehla neuron model 1943 me McCulloch aur Pitts ne propose kiya tha.

Researchers:

  • Warren McCulloch → Neuroscientist

  • Walter Pitts → Logician

Inka idea tha:

Human brain me neurons complex processing karte hain. Kya hum ek simple mathematical model bana sakte hain jo neuron jaise decision le sake?

Isliye unhone simplified computational neuron banaya.

2. Basic Idea of McCulloch-Pitts Neuron

Neuron ka kaam:

  1. Inputs lena

  2. Unhe combine karna

  3. Ek decision output dena

Inputs

  • Inputs binary (0 ya 1) hote hain.

  • Matlab yes/no type information.

Example inputs:

  • Kya baarish ho rahi hai?

  • Kya main free hoon?

  • Kya movie achhi hai?

Har input:

  • 1 → True / Yes

  • 0 → False / No

3. Structure of McCulloch-Pitts Neuron

Neuron ke 3 main parts hote hain:

1️⃣ Inputs

x₁, x₂, x₃ … xₙ

2️⃣ Aggregation function (g)

  • Ye sab inputs ko combine karta hai

  • Usually sum hota hai

g(x)=x1+x2+x3+...+xng(x)=x1​+x2​+x3​+...+xn​

Kyuki inputs 0 ya 1 hain:

  • g(x) = number of inputs that are ON

Example:

InputsSum g(x)
1 0 12
1 1 13
0 0 11

3️⃣ Activation Function (f)

Ye decide karta hai:

neuron fire karega ya nahi

Rule:

f(g(x))={1if g(x)≥θ0otherwise

yaha:

something like ki 2 se jyada h toh fire krega nhi toh nhi krega fire  2 out of 3 

θ (theta) = Threshold

4. Threshold Concept

Threshold decide karta hai:

kitne inputs true hone chahiye output = 1 ke liye

Example:

Case 1

Threshold = 2

Output = 1 only if

g(x) ≥ 2

Matlab kam se kam 2 inputs ON hone chahiye

Real life analogy

Movie dekhne ka decision.

Inputs:

  • Good movie?

  • Free time?

  • Not raining?

Agar threshold = 2   

Matlab:

inme se 2 conditions true hongi tabhi movie dekhne jaoge

Personality analogy

Threshold depend karta hai:

Lazy person

  • bahut conditions chahiye

  • threshold high

Outgoing person

  • thodi conditions par hi ready

  • threshold low

5. Types of Inputs

Neuron me do type ke inputs ho sakte hain:

1️⃣ Excitatory Inputs

Ye positive inputs hote hain.

Agar ye 1 ho jaye to neuron fire hone me help karte hain.

Example:

  • good movie

  • free time

  • weekend

2️⃣ Inhibitory Inputs

Ye negative inputs hote hain.

Rule:

Agar inhibitory input = 1
To output always 0

Chahe baaki inputs kuch bhi ho.

Example

Decision: Movie dekhne jana

Inputs:

  • Good movie

  • Free time

  • Rain

  • High fever (inhibitory)

Agar fever = 1

➡ movie cancel

6. Mathematical Definition (Without Inhibitory Inputs)


Agar inhibitory inputs nahi hain to:

Aggregation

g(x)=x1+x2+...+xn

Activation

f(g(x))={1if g(x)≥θ0otherwise

7. Boolean Functions using McCulloch-Pitts Neuron

Kyuki:

  • inputs binary

  • output binary

Isliye ye Boolean functions implement kar sakta hai

Example Boolean functions:

  • AND

  • OR

  • NOT

  • NOR

9. Function Implement Karna Kya Hota Hai?

Agar koi model same output produce kare
jo original Boolean function deta hai.

To hum bolte hain:

Model ne us function ko implement / represent kar diya.


8. Boolean Function Implement Karna

Implement karna matlab:

neuron ka output exactly same ho jo Boolean function deta hai

Example:

AND function

100

Agar neuron same output deta hai to:

neuron AND function represent karta hai

9. AND Function Implementation

Assume:

Inputs = 3

x1 x2 x3

AND ka rule:

output = 1 only if all inputs = 1

Aggregation:

g(x) = x1 + x2 + x3

Maximum value:

3

Output 1 chahiye only when:

g(x) = 3

Isliye:

θ = 3

Correct → AND implemented

10. OR Function Implementation

OR rule:

agar koi bhi input = 1 ho to output = 1

Aggregation:

g(x) = x1 + x2 + x3

Condition:

g(x) ≥ 1

Isliye:

θ = 1

Check

01011
10011
11131

Correct → OR implemented

11. NOT Function

NOT function:

xOutput
01
10

Isme:

  • input inhibitory banate hain

Rule:

Agar input = 1
→ output = 0

Agar input = 0
→ output = 1

Threshold set:

θ = 0

12. NOR Function

NOR = NOT(OR)

Truth table:

x1x2Output
001
010
100
110

Isko bhi threshold logic se implement kar sakte hain.

x1 + x2 > 0 ; 

13. Key Idea of McCulloch-Pitts Neuron

Neuron basically:

1️⃣ Inputs ko sum karta hai
2️⃣ Threshold se compare karta hai
3️⃣ Output decide karta hai

Mathematically:

y={1x1+x2+...+xn≥θ0otherwise

Isko kehte hain:

Threshold Logic

14. Important Observation

Ye model:

✔ AND implement kar sakta hai
✔ OR implement kar sakta hai
✔ NOT implement kar sakta hai

Next question:

Kya har Boolean function is neuron se implement ho sakta hai?

Iska answer samajhne ke liye:

➡ geometric interpretation use karte hain
➡ jo next topic hai.

  • 2 inhibitory inputs

  • threshold θ = 0

4️⃣ Big Question

Ab ek important question aata hai:

Kya har Boolean function McCulloch-Pitts neuron se represent ho sakta hai?

Abhi tak hum sirf sum + threshold dekh rahe the.

Is question ko samajhne ke liye lecture me geometric interpretation introduce kiya gaya.

5️⃣ Geometric Interpretation

Example inequality:

x1 + x2 ≥ 1

Agar temporary inequality hata dein:

x1 + x2 = 1

Ye kya hai?

👉 Line ki equation

6️⃣ Line ke Points

Equation:

x1 + x2 = 1

Is line par kuch points:

(0,1)
(1,0)

Kyuki:

0 + 1 = 1
1 + 0 = 1

7️⃣ Boolean World

Normal 2D plane me infinite points hote hain.

Lekin Boolean inputs me sirf 4 points possible hain:

(0,0)
(0,1)
(1,0)
(1,1)

Hum sirf inhi points ki care karte hain.

8️⃣ McCulloch-Pitts Neuron Actually Kya Kar Raha Hai

Rule:

x1 + x2 ≥ 1

Matlab:

Agar point line par ya line ke upar hai

Output = 1

Agar point line ke neeche hai

Output = 0

Points Check

pointsumoutput
0,000
0,111
1,011
1,121

9️⃣ Half Space Concept

Line plane ko 2 parts me divide karti hai.

Positive Half Space

Jo points satisfy karte hain:

x1 + x2 ≥ 1

Output:

1

Points:

(0,1)
(1,0)
(1,1)

Negative Half Space

Jo satisfy karte hain:

x1 + x2 < 1

Output:

0

Point:

(0,0)

🔑 Important Idea

McCulloch-Pitts neuron basically:

Ek line draw karta hai

Aur input space ko 2 parts me divide karta hai:

Positive region
Negative region

1️⃣0️⃣ Example: AND Function

Equation:

x1 + x2 ≥ 2

Temporary equality:

x1 + x2 = 2

Line pass karegi:

(1,1)

Check:

pointsumoutput
0,000
0,110
1,010
1,121

Sirf (1,1) positive half space me hai.

1️⃣1️⃣ Tautology Function

Tautology ka matlab:

Output hamesha 1

Threshold:

θ = 0

Equation:

x1 + x2 = 0

Is case me:

Saare points positive half space me aa jaate hain.

To:

Output = 1 for all inputs

1️⃣2️⃣ Main Takeaway

McCulloch-Pitts neuron:

Input space ko ek line se divide karta hai

Isko bolte hain:

Linear Decision Boundary

1️⃣3️⃣ Agar Inputs 3 Ho

2D me:

decision boundary = line

3D me:

decision boundary = plane

OR Function Example (3 inputs)

Truth rule:

Sirf:

(0,0,0)

par output 0

Baaki sab par 1

Plane:

x1 + x2 + x3 = 1

Is plane ke:

  • neeche → (0,0,0)

  • upar → baaki 7 points

1️⃣4️⃣ Hyperplane Concept

Agar inputs  n ho:

Decision boundary:

hyperplane

General equation:

x1 + x2 + x3 + ... + xn = θ

1️⃣5️⃣ Linear Separability

Definition:

Agar ek line / plane / hyperplane se:

  • output = 0 wale points

  • output = 1 wale points

alag kiye ja sakte hain

To function linearly separable hai.

1️⃣6️⃣ Final Conclusion

Single McCulloch-Pitts neuron sirf un Boolean functions ko represent kar sakta hai jo:

linearly separable

ho.

1️⃣7️⃣ Next Big Question

Lecture ka next topic:

Kya koi Boolean function aisa bhi hai
jo linearly separable nahi hai?

Answer:

Haan

Sabse famous example:

XOR function

Jise single neuron implement nahi kar sakta.



Baaki phases baad me continue kar sakte hain.

📘 Perceptron Lecture Notes (Hinglish) — Phase 1

1. Story so far (Ab tak kya padha)

Ab tak hum McCulloch-Pitts (MP) neuron ke baare me padh rahe the.

Usme kuch limitations thi:

❓ Problem 1: Non-Boolean Inputs

Ab tak inputs sirf boolean (0 ya 1) the.

Example:

  • x1 = 0 or 1

  • x2 = 0 or 1

Question:
Agar inputs real numbers ho (jaise 0.7, 2.3, -1 etc.) toh kya hoga?

Iska solution perceptron me milega.

❓ Problem 2: Threshold manually calculate karna

MP neuron me hume threshold khud calculate karna padta tha.

Example:

AND function ke liye:

x1 + x2 ≥ 2 → output = 1

Yeh humne hand calculation se find kiya.

Lekin problem:

Real world me:

  • inputs bahut zyada honge

  • functions complex honge

Toh manually threshold find karna impractical ho jayega.

Isliye hume learning algorithm chahiye.

❓ Problem 3: All inputs equal importance

MP neuron me:

Output = x1 + x2 + x3 + ...

Sab inputs equal importance ke the.

Real life me aisa nahi hota.

Example: Movie dekhni hai ya nahi.

Factors:

  • Actor

  • Director

  • Genre

  • Reviews

Sab equal importance nahi hote.

Example:

  • Director Christopher Nolan → bahut important

  • Genre → medium

  • Actor → less important

Isliye weights ki zarurat hoti hai.

❓ Problem 4: Non-linearly separable functions

MP neuron sirf linearly separable functions implement kar sakta hai.

Example:

✔ AND
✔ OR

Lekin kuch functions:

❌ XOR

Line se separate nahi hote.

Isko bhi aage explore karenge.

2. Perceptron ka Introduction

Perceptron ko propose kiya tha:

👨‍🔬 Frank Rosenblatt (1958)

Later analysis:

👨‍🔬 Marvin Minsky
👨‍🔬 Seymour Papert

Year: 1969

3. Perceptron vs MP Neuron

Main differences:

1️⃣ Inputs real ho sakte hain

MP neuron:

xi ∈ {0,1}

Perceptron:

xi ∈ Real numbers

Example:

x1 = 0.8
x2 = -1.2
x3 = 2.5

2️⃣ Weights add ho gaye

Har input ka ek weight hota hai.

w1 → weight of x1
w2 → weight of x2

Equation:


Isse pata chalta hai kaunsa input important hai.

3️⃣ Learning algorithm

Perceptron me:

Weights automatically learn ho sakte hain data se.

Manual calculation ki zarurat nahi.

4. Perceptron Model Equation

Perceptron ka main rule:

Output = 1 if weighted sum ≥ threshold

[
y = 1 \quad if \quad \sum_{i=1}^{n} w_i x_i \ge \theta
]

Otherwise:

[
y = 0
]

Iska matlab

  1. Sab inputs ko weights se multiply karo

  2. Sabka sum lo

  3. Threshold se compare karo

5. Equation ko rewrite karna

Original equation:

[
\sum_{i=1}^{n} w_i x_i \ge \theta
]

Ab θ ko left side le aate hain.

[
\sum_{i=1}^{n} w_i x_i - \theta \ge 0
]

6. Bias introduce karna

Ab ek trick use karte hain.

Ek imaginary input add karte hain:

x0 = 1

Aur weight define karte hain:

w0 = -θ

Ab equation ban jayegi:

[
\sum_{i=0}^{n} w_i x_i \ge 0
]

Ab model simple ho gaya

w0x0 + w1x1 + w2x2 + ... + wnxn ≥ 0

Yaha:

x0 = 1

Isliye:

w0 = bias

7. Bias kya hota hai?

Bias = initial tendency / prior belief

Example: Movie decision.

Inputs:

  • Actor = Matt Damon

  • Director = Nolan

  • Genre = Thriller

Agar koi person sirf Nolan movies dekhta hai, toh:

w_director → very high

Agar koi movie buff hai (sab movies dekhta hai):

threshold = low

Agar koi bahut picky hai:

threshold = high

Isliye bias personality / prior preference ko represent karta hai.

8. Boolean functions kyu important hain?

Real world problems ko boolean inputs me convert kar sakte hain.

Example:

Movie decision features:

FeatureBoolean
Matt Damon actor0/1
Christopher Nolan director0/1
Thriller genre0/1
Comedy genre0/1

Output:

Watch movie = 1
Don't watch = 0

Phase 1 Summary

Perceptron ne MP neuron ki problems solve ki:

✔ Real inputs allow kiye
✔ Weights add kiye
✔ Learning algorithm introduce kiya
✔ Bias concept introduce kiya

Equation:

[
\sum_{i=0}^{n} w_i x_i ≥ 0
]

Perceptron:

w1x1 + w2x2 ≥ threshold

9️⃣ Geometry View

Equation:

w1x1 + w2x2 + w0 = 0

Ye equation represent karti hai:

ek line

Is line ke:

ek side → output 1
dusri side → output 0

🔟 Example Boundary

Suppose equation:

Ye ek line hai.

Is line ke:

green side → output 1

Agar saare correct points correct side me hain:

error = 0

1️⃣1️⃣ Galat Boundary

Kabhi boundary galat bhi ho sakti hai.

Example:

kuch positive points negative side me chale gaye

Tab:

error > 0

Machine learning ka goal:

error minimize karna

1️⃣2️⃣ Error Concept

📘 Perceptron Convergence Proof — Detailed Notes (Phase 1)

1. Linear Separability (Definition)

We have two sets of points:

  • P → positive class

  • N → negative class

Each point is an n-dimensional vector:

x=(x1,x2,...,xn)x = (x_1, x_2, ..., x_n)x=(x1​,x2​,...,xn​)

Definition

The sets P and N are linearly separable if there exist numbers

w0,w1,w2,...,wnw_0, w_1, w_2, ..., w_nw0​,w1​,w2​,...,wn​

such that:

For every x∈Px \in Px∈P

w1x1+w2x2+...+wnxn≥w0w_1x_1 + w_2x_2 + ... + w_nx_n \geq w_0w1​x1​+w2​x2​+...+wn​xn​≥w0​

For every x∈Nx \in Nx∈N

w1x1+w2x2+...+wnxn<w0w_1x_1 + w_2x_2 + ... + w_nx_n < w_0w1​x1​+w2​x2​+...+wn​xn​<w0​

Meaning

There exists a hyperplane:

wTx=w0w^T x = w_0wTx=w0​

that separates the data.

So:

  • points in P → one side

  • points in N → other side

Geometric intuition

In:

  • 2D → line

  • 3D → plane

  • nD → hyperplane

Example:

Positive points:  o o o
Negative points:  x x x

A line separates them.

2. The Perceptron Convergence Theorem

Statement

If:

1️⃣ Dataset is finite
2️⃣ Dataset is linearly separable

Then:

The Perceptron Learning Algorithm will update weights only a finite number of times.

Eventually it will find a weight vector w that separates the data.

Why this theorem matters

Without this theorem we could worry that:

update → update → update → update → forever

Weights might keep oscillating.

The theorem proves:

✔ Updates cannot continue forever
✔ Algorithm must converge

3. What the Algorithm Does

Perceptron update rule:

If a point is misclassified:

Positive mistake:

w=w+xw = w + xw=w+x

Negative mistake:

w=w−xw = w - xw=w−x

Concern:

Maybe:

fix point A
→ break point B
→ fix point B
→ break point A
→ repeat forever

The proof shows:

❌ This infinite loop cannot happen if data is linearly separable.

4. Key Trick Used in the Proof

Instead of handling:

positive points
negative points

the proof converts everything into positive points.

This simplifies math.

5. Key Observation

If

x∈Nx \in Nx∈N

then

−x∈P-x \in P−x∈P

Why?

If

wTx<0w^T x < 0wTx<0

then

wT(−x)>0w^T(-x) > 0wT(−x)>0

So a negative point becomes positive when flipped.

6. Constructing a New Dataset

Define:

N−={−x∣x∈N}N^{-} = \{-x \mid x \in N\}N−={−x∣x∈N}

This means:

Take every negative point and negate it.

Example:

Original N:  (2,3)

N⁻: (-2,-3)

New dataset

P′=P∪N−P' = P \cup N^{-}P′=P∪N−

Now:

Every point in P′ should satisfy

wTp≥0w^T p \geq 0wTp≥0

We only check one condition now.

Why this helps

Original condition:

Positive → w·x ≥ 0
Negative → w·x < 0

After transformation:

All points → w·p ≥ 0

Much simpler.

7. Simplified Perceptron Algorithm

Dataset:

P′ = P ∪ N⁻

Algorithm:

1️⃣ Initialize weight vector w randomly

2️⃣ Repeat until convergence

Pick random p∈P′p \in P'p∈P′

Check:

wTp≥0w^T p \geq 0wTp≥0

If satisfied → do nothing

If violated:

w=w+pw = w + pw=w+p

Important difference

Original perceptron had two updates:

w = w + x
w = w - x

Now we only have:

w = w + p

Because all labels are +1.

8. Normalization Assumption

For mathematical convenience:

Every point is normalized.

Meaning:

∣∣p∣∣=1||p|| = 1∣∣p∣∣=1

So:

p=p∣∣p∣∣p = \frac{p}{||p||}p=∣∣p∣∣p​

Why normalization is allowed

Because if

wTp≥0w^T p \geq 0wTp≥0

then

wTp∣∣p∣∣≥0w^T \frac{p}{||p||} \geq 0wT∣∣p∣∣p​≥0

So the condition does not change.

15️⃣ Loss Function Definition

Prediction aur true value ka difference.

  • f(x) → model prediction

  • y→ true value

Loss = squared error between prediction and actual value.


16️⃣ Why 12\frac{1}{2} is Added

Loss formula:

12(f(x)−y)2\frac{1}{2}(f(x)-y)^2

Reason:

Mathematical convenience.

Derivative lene par:

2×122 \times \frac{1}{2}

cancel ho jata hai.

Important:

Scaling factor change karne se minimum point change nahi hota.

17️⃣ Goal of Gradient Descent

Gradient descent ke liye hume chahiye:

∂L∂w\frac{\partial L}{\partial w}

Aur

∂L∂b\frac{\partial L}{\partial b}

Meaning:

Loss function ka derivative with respect to parameters.

18️⃣ Partial Derivative w.r.t www

Loss:

L=12(f(x)−y)2L = \frac{1}{2}(f(x)-y)^2L=21​(f(x)−y)2

Derivative:

Chain rule apply hota hai.

Step:

∂L∂w=∂∂w(12(f(x)−y)2)\frac{\partial L}{\partial w} = \frac{\partial}{\partial w} \left(\frac{1}{2}(f(x)-y)^2\right)∂w∂L​=∂w∂​(21​(f(x)−y)2)

Chain rule:

=(f(x)−y)∂f(x)∂w= (f(x)-y) \frac{\partial f(x)}{\partial w}=(f(x)−y)∂w∂f(x)​

Because:

Derivative of u2u^2u2 = 2u⋅du/dw2u \cdot du/dw2u⋅du/dw

Aur 1/21/21/2 cancel ho jata hai.

19️⃣ Derivative of Sigmoid

Sigmoid function:

f(x)=11+e−(wx+b)f(x)=\frac{1}{1+e^{-(wx+b)}}f(x)=1+e−(wx+b)1​

Iska derivative property hota hai:

dfdz=f(x)(1−f(x))\frac{df}{dz} = f(x)(1-f(x))dzdf​=f(x)(1−f(x))

Where:

z=wx+bz = wx+bz=wx+b

20️⃣ Applying Chain Rule

Since

z=wx+bz = wx+bz=wx+b

Derivative:

dzdw=x\frac{dz}{dw}=xdwdz​=x

Final result:

∂f(x)∂w=f(x)(1−f(x))x\frac{\partial f(x)}{\partial w} = f(x)(1-f(x))x∂w∂f(x)​=f(x)(1−f(x))x

21️⃣ Final Gradient Formula for www

Substitute back:

∂L∂w=(f(x)−y)f(x)(1−f(x))x\frac{\partial L}{\partial w} = (f(x)-y)f(x)(1-f(x))x∂w∂L​=(f(x)−y)f(x)(1−f(x))x

Ye gradient formula hai.

22️⃣ Gradient for bbb

Similar process follow hota hai.

Difference:

dzdb=1\frac{dz}{db}=1dbdz​=1

Isliye final formula:

∂L∂b=(f(x)−y)f(x)(1−f(x))\frac{\partial L}{\partial b} = (f(x)-y)f(x)(1-f(x))∂b∂L​=(f(x)−y)f(x)(1−f(x))

Note:

bbb derivative me x term nahi hota.

Derivative rule:

Derivative of sum = sum of derivatives.

So gradient becomes:

∑(f(xi)−yi)f(xi)(1−f(xi))xi\sum (f(x_i)-y_i)f(x_i)(1-f(x_i))x_i∑(f(xi​)−yi​)f(xi​)(1−f(xi​))xi​
